entry.title: Do you know how to implement, maintain, and enhance your SAP Service and Asset Manager?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing SAP Service and Asset Manager learning journey.

Overview
This Learning Journey provides an in-depth understanding of SAP Service and Asset Manager.
The SAP Service and Asset Manager mobile app provides access to all services and information that maintenance and field technicians need to efficiently and safely do their job, no matter where they are, using smartphones or tablets - even when they're offline.

Learning objectives
After completing this intermediate level learning journey, Implementation Consultants will obtain product knowledge and they will be able to understand the application architecture.
Also they will be prepared to install, setup OOB Service and Asset Manager and connect from client end-to end.
In addition they will learn how to setup backend Standard Configuration, Metadata Deployment and Basic and Advanced Extensions.
They will understand and know about how to utilize the support options available as well.

Prerequisites

  • Discovering Business Processes in SAP Intelligent Asset Management

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Leading Ledger Selection for Parallel Accounting in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear @Shakeel_Ahmed 

It is a very comprehensive article and I think this blog must be highlighted to everyone.

BTW: Could you help provide the advice for the situation that we would like to change our mind?

1. If we selected 1GA (Ledger 2L) already, but we would like to maintain only 1 Ledger (0L) for each company in our system. Can we still do that? Or we need to deactivate 1GA, which I believe, we cannot.

2. If we started and go-live in our project without 1GA, i.e. we have only 1 ledger for each company. Later, we have acquired a new company where different account principles needed or fiscal year variants needed. Can we still activate 1GA to support this new company?

To me, it seems that we should activate 1GA even without current requirements (to be safe than sorry). Please correct me if i am wrong.

Best Regards

entry.title: FM ENQUEUE_READ for object EBAN
entry.content:
Hello everybody,

We want to check a certain purchase requisition with function module ENQUEUE_READ.
In transaction SM12 the argument is mandt + requ.nr + #####

Question: How do we have to fill parameter GARG to get a result?
Input without #### doesn't work. Input with ##### doesn't work either.
mandt + requ.nr + * works, but only when testing the FM, but not in a report.

Best Regards,
Andreas

entry.title: 从0开始的SAP世界 Day1
entry.content:
1.ERP是什么？

全称：Enterprise Resource Planning。

中文：企业资源规划

解释：整合企业的各个部门（财务、销售、生产、库存等），让企业高效运转。可以理解成企业的大脑。

核心功能：财务管理，库存管理，销售与客户记录，生产管理，采购管理等。

代表性的ERP系统：SAP、Saleforce、Oracle、Microsoft Dynamics 365、Workday

SAP：德语“Systeme, Anwendungen und Produkte”，意思是“系统、应用与产品”。自 1972 年创立以来，SAP 一直是全球企业管理软件领域的领导者。

2.SAP的各个模块

entry.title: Document Information Extraction Free Tier Now Includes Premium Edition Capabilities
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce an exciting upgrade to the Free Tier service plan for Document Information Extraction! As of now, the Free Tier option on SAP BTP offers the advanced capabilities of the Premium Edition plan. This includes access to cutting-edge Generative AI features driven by the latest advancements in the space of Large Language Models (LLMs).

Enhanced Capabilities Now Available

The integration of the Premium Edition capabilities into the Free Tier plan means that you can now leverage all the powerful features such as:

- Auto Extraction without model training: Automatically extract business-relevant entities from documents without the need for extensive model training.

- Instant Learning Based on User Corrections: The platform now learns and improves in real-time based on user inputs and corrections.

- Embedded Document Classification: Classify documents seamlessly with embedded classification capabilities.

These features enable businesses to automate and accelerate document processing, improving efficiency and accuracy without additional costs.

Getting Started with the Enhanced Free Tier

To start using the upgraded Free Tier service plan for Document Information Extraction, follow these steps:

  1. Register and create an account in SAP BTP: This account will grant you access to the Document Information Extraction service with the Premium Edition capabilities.
  2. Explore the Free Tier Quota: The free tier offers a quota of 50 uploaded document pages per tenant within a rolling period of 30 days. Make the most of this allocation to test and build your scenarios.

Tutorials to Get Enabled

We have prepared tutorials to help you set up and utilize the Free Tier plan of the Document Information Extraction service:

- Get an Account on SAP BTP to Try Out Free Tier Service Plans: Step-by-step instructions to register and create your SAP BTP account.

- Use Free Tier to Set Up Account for Document Information Extraction and Go to Application: A guide to setting up the Document Information Extraction service and accessing the application.

These resources will ensure a smooth transition from testing to productive usage, empowering you to fully explore the capabilities of the Premium Edition within the Free Tier plan.

Conclusion

This upgrade is a testament to our commitment to providing high-value tools and services accessible to all businesses. By integrating Generative AI capabilities into the Free Tier plan, we aim to democratize access to advanced document processing technologies, enabling you to harness the full potential of SAP AI Services.

entry.title: Re: Error with context.html file

We were having some issues with the Help content not appearing for a particular app. I thought deleting and regenerating the context file would work, but doing that also created a new published link...The URL entered into SM30 was the one from the first context.html file, hence the 404 error. BartRhoten, I think this is what you are saying with your reply.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2411
entry.content:
This month's bumper update is filled with exciting innovations to elevate your asset performance management. We'll start this edition with the latest highlights for asset strategy, then move on to the exciting first release of industrial systems, providing you with multiple perspectives on technical systems. We'll then introduce you to our new asset health features with embedded IoT and visual condition monitoring capabilities using images and AI. Let's dive in!

 

Asset Risk Assessment

Mark an impact as relevant or not relevant in a Risk and Criticality Assessment

Effective asset management requires precise and adaptable evaluation of risks and their impacts. The ability to exclude non-essential impacts in Risk and Criticality assessments provides organizations with the flexibility to tailor assessments to specific operational needs. Whether managing assets across diverse locations or varying technical objects, this functionality ensures assessments focus solely on what truly matters, improving accuracy and decision-making.

Key Benefits of Impact Exclusion

  • Tailored Assessments: By excluding irrelevant impacts, you can create focused evaluations that align with specific business contexts, such as regional differences or unique technical object requirements.
  • Improved Clarity: Marked exclusions are clearly labeled within the assessment, helping teams concentrate on the most critical factors and document reasons for exclusions.
  • Accurate Risk Scores: Excluded impacts are automatically omitted from calculations, ensuring that the overall risk score reflects only the relevant high-risk elements without distortion.

This feature empowers businesses to:

  • Streamline Assessments: Focus only on essential impacts to reduce noise and enhance decision-making.
  • Ensure Consistency: Apply exclusions uniformly across similar objects or scenarios, improving the reliability of risk scores.
  • Save Time and Effort: Use templates and Excel integration to efficiently manage assessments at scale.

By enabling teams to concentrate on critical factors and eliminate unnecessary data, businesses can enhance their risk evaluations and achieve more actionable insights. Watch the video to find out more.

 

Asset Reliability Engineering

Mark a strategy assessment for classes as a 'template'

Consistency, control, and traceability are essential for effective strategy implementation in asset management. Marking a strategy assessment for classes as a template helps standardize processes, improve governance, and simplify reassessment efforts. Templates serve as a central reference, allowing organizations to align strategy assessments with operational goals while maintaining flexibility and oversight.

Key Business Benefits of Using Templates

  • Central Governance: Templates provide a consistent framework for assessments, reducing variability and supporting standardization.
  • Improved Traceability: The ability to track copies of Templates and linked assessments makes it easier to manage updates and maintain alignment.
  • Simplified Processes: Templates streamline the creation and replication of assessments, saving time and ensuring uniformity.

How Templates Support Governance

Templates are designed to be used as references rather than directly implemented, ensuring better control over assessments:

  • No Technical Object Assignments: Templates cannot link to technical objects, preserving their role as reusable standards.
  • Controlled Recommendations: Recommendations associated with Templates are always in a "Released" status to maintain consistency.

Visibility into Template usage is another key feature:

  • Copy Tracking: Filters and the Template Copy Count feature allow users to monitor how many assessments are derived from a Template, making it easier to apply updates when needed.

Business Impact

Using Templates for strategy assessments allows organizations to:

  • Maintain Consistency: Ensure assessments follow a standard approach without additional effort.
  • Simplify Updates: Quickly identify and revise assessments that are based on a Template, ensuring changes are applied consistently.
  • Save Time: Replicate standardized assessments efficiently, speeding up processes while maintaining quality.

Watch the video to see it in action:

 

Reset recommendation through authorization control

Efficient asset management requires the ability to adapt recommendations as needs evolve. Now, authorized users can reset statuses like 'Implemented' to 'Implementation in Process' or 'Released' to 'In Process', enabling updates to technical objects, maintenance plans, and more.

Key Benefits

  • Flexibility: Adjust recommendations to align with changing requirements.
  • Governance: Role-based controls ensure secure, authorized updates.
  • Transparency: Comments for status changes enhance traceability.

By streamlining rework processes and enabling updates under strict controls, this feature enhances agility and accuracy in recommendation management.

 

Asset Information and Data Management

Manage Industrial Systems

Efficiently managing complex systems is a critical challenge in asset-heavy industries like Oil & Gas and Railways. To address this, we have a new application Manage Industrial Systems, a powerful master data concept designed to simplify and enhance asset management by providing multiple perspectives on technical systems.

What Are Industrial Systems?

Based on the ISO 81346 standard, an Industrial System is a collection of interrelated objects defined within a specific context. It provides three distinct views, or aspects, of technical systems:

  • Functional View: Focused on operational roles and processes.
  • Product-Related View: Centered on the components and equipment.
  • Location-Based View: Highlighting physical and geographical contexts.

How It Works

Using the Manage Industrial Systems application in SAP Asset Performance Management, users can create and model system structures, linking them seamlessly to technical objects such as functional locations and equipment in SAP ERP. This integration ensures that Industrial Systems are fully aligned with existing asset structures, eliminating redundancy and enhancing accessibility.

Business Benefits

  • Enhanced Flexibility: Move beyond a single leading structure to view and manage assets from multiple perspectives.
  • Operational Efficiency: Simplify navigation and access to assets, reducing time spent on asset identification and management.
  • Seamless Integration: Align technical systems across SAP ERP and Asset Performance Management, ensuring consistency and continuity in asset data.
  • Improved Decision-Making: Access to varied perspectives allows for better insights and strategic planning.
  • With Industrial Systems, SAP Asset Performance Management empowers industries to tackle the complexity of asset management with a structured, adaptable approach, driving operational efficiency and better asset utilization.

Watch the video for a quick introduction to Industrial Systems:

 

Manage Reference Designator Libraries: The Foundation for Efficient Industrial Systems

As an administrator, creating a clear Reference Designator Scheme is essential for managing industrial systems effectively. This scheme uses object class codes to unambiguously identify each system aspect, serving as the foundation for building the system hierarchy. By assigning unique Reference Designator codes to each system element, you ensure clarity and structure, making it easier to organize and manage complex assets across the system.

Prerequisites in the Configuration Manager

Before using the Manage Reference Designator Libraries application, certain configurations need to be completed in the Configuration Manager application:

  • Industry Domains: Define the areas where your industrial systems operate (e.g., Oil & Gas, Railways). While predefined domains are available, you can create custom domains as needed.
  • Reference Designator Sources: Set the reference designator standards, such as ISO, DIN, IEEE, or ANSI, that will be used to structure the system.
  • Aspects: Specify the different ways to structure your industrial systems (e.g., Function, Product, or Location), allowing you to filter system elements based on these categories.
  • Relationship Types: Define how system elements are related to one another (e.g., "realized" or "located"). These relationships help organize the system hierarchy.

Once these configurations are complete, you are ready to use the Manage Reference Designator Libraries application to build and maintain your system structure.

Key features of the Manage Reference Designator Libraries application

  • Set Up Reference Designators: Using the Reference Designator Scheme, administrators can create a system hierarchy where each element is identified using a unique Reference Designator code. This ensures clarity, consistency, and ease of management.
  • Create and Maintain Hierarchies: With the Reference Designator Library, you can build and manage system hierarchies by linking system elements and their relationships. This organization ensures that assets are logically structured and easily navigable.
  • Import and Export Data: The app supports importing and exporting object classes via Excel. This feature makes it simple to update, add, or modify system elements without manually entering data, saving time and reducing errors.

Business Benefits

  • Improved Organizational Clarity: With a clear system hierarchy based on structured Reference Designators, your team can quickly identify and manage assets, reducing confusion and errors.
  • Increased Flexibility: The ability to customize domains, standards, and aspects gives you the flexibility to tailor the system to your specific industry needs, ensuring your asset management system adapts to changing requirements.
  • Enhanced Efficiency: Streamlined data import and export processes ensure quick updates to the system, while pre-configured elements save time during system setup and maintenance.
  • Global Compliance: By adhering to international standards, you ensure that your system is compliant with global regulations, helping maintain consistency and reliability across multiple regions.

The Manage Reference Designator Libraries application is essential for building and managing efficient industrial systems. By providing a structured approach to identifying and organizing system elements, it enhances clarity, flexibility, and operational efficiency, while ensuring compliance with global standards. Watch this video to see how it works:

 

Asset Health Monitoring

Visual Condition Monitoring Using Images and AI

Effective asset management is critical to minimizing downtime and ensuring operational efficiency. With visual condition monitoring using images and AI, you can proactively identify potential issues and take timely action. With our current beta release, you can:

  • Configure visual inspection indicators
  • View visual inspection results in the indicator monitoring chart via Explore Technical Objects
  • Configure alert types, data sets and rules
  • Process alerts from the alert lists using supporting visual images providing feedback to improve AI-generated results.

Watch this space for an upcoming dedicated blog on visual condition monitoring using images and AI but here is a quick introduction.

Configure visual inspection indicators

To do visual condition monitoring using images and AI, define the indicator as a "visual inspection indicator". A picture icon appears on the indicator, making it easy to differentiate visual inspection indicators from other metrics.

View visual inspection results in the Indicator Monitoring chart via Explore Technical Objects

The system displays measurements and images in the indicator monitoring chart with visualized minimum and maximum thresholds. If a measurement exceeds the threshold, an alert is triggered, enabling early issue detection.

Configure alert types, data sets and rules

Once alert types, data sets and rules are set up, the asset conditions are evaluated based on the defined threshold and schedule.

Process alerts from the alert lists using supporting visual images

If a critical condition, like low thickness, is met, an alert is generated, ensuring timely attention and minimizing maintenance delays. The alert contains an image of the part in question, supporting reliability engineers in deciding on the next maintenance steps.

By combining visual condition monitoring using images with automated alerts, you can streamline your condition monitoring process, improve asset management efficiency, and proactively address maintenance needs. Watch the video to see the end-to-end process.

 

Condition-Based Monitoring with Alerts and Embedded IoT

We're introducing new and improved functionalities for users of Embedded IoT tenants. Watch this space for a dedicated blog but here's a quick introduction to the new features.

Manage Data Sets Application

This application allows users to define data sets, which can then be consumed by the configured rules. Data sets act as the basis for defining aggregations and their respective time windows, enabling their use both during rule design and at runtime. The same data set can be reused for monitoring and derivation rules.

New Rules Design-Time in Embedded IoT Tenants

One of the most significant updates in this release is the introduction of a new rules design-time for Embedded IoT tenants. This includes:

  • Monitoring rules - to monitor the condition of a technical object.
  • Derivation rules - to enable the calculation of indicator values, including their timestamps, by deriving them from other indicator values based on specified conditions.

Scheduling Flexibility

The new rules can be configured to run based on a defined schedule. This flexibility ensures that rules are executed at optimal times, enhancing performance and efficiency. Currently, the most frequent scheduling interval supported is every 5 minutes.

Dynamic Indicator Chart

There's a new indicator chart which visualizes values across time series ranges with the minimum, maximum, and average values. This is useful when dealing with datasets that have significant fluctuations or disparities.

Simplified Data Integration

Embedded IoT introduces a streamlined Data Integration channel, ideal if you have extensive data lakes or batch data requirements. This feature supports large data transfers with pre-configured intervals (e.g., daily or hourly) and structured file ingestion, simplifying the management of time series data. This is perfect if you are relying on big data analytics, as it enables seamless handling of large datasets without the need for real-time processing.

The file upload interface allows for the efficient, compressed transfer of time series data, this documentation details the steps to set up a process to ingest compressed files. Users can easily access the necessary information for preparing data packages and monitoring processing status via APM's API interfaces. With Embedded IoT, assets are automatically enabled to retrieve time series data, eliminating the need for manual 'IoT Sync'. This simplifies connectivity to time series data sources, enhancing business value by optimizing the data integration process.

Subscription Updates: Important Details for Users

If you have an existing subscription to SAP Asset Performance Management with the material code 8012342, there are some essential updates to note.

Under the current material code, you can only use the Embedded IoT (EIoT) tenants for testing purposes.

Switching to Productive Usage

To transition to using EIoT tenants for productive purposes, please get in touch with your SAP account team. They will assist you in switching to the latest material, ensuring you have the proper setup for your business needs.

Further Information

For additional details, please refer to SAP Note 3523368. This document provides comprehensive information on the changes and how to proceed.

Watch this video to see a step-by-step process:

Keep In Touch

Here's a quick reminder how to keep in touch with all things APM:

  • Check out all past and present Intelligent Asset Management webcasts here. If you register for an IAM webcast, you will be automatically subscribed to our IAM Community Engagement Newsletter. 
  • Check some of our recent blogs:
    • Why Failure-Mode Focus Matters in Intelligent Asset Management 
    • SAP Asset Performance Management Embedding Cumulocity IoT to Drive Innovations in IoT and AI
    • How AI-based Anomaly Detection empowers reliability engineers in SAP Asset Performance Management
  • Subscribe to What's New on the SAP Help Portal
  • Submit or vote for the improvement you would like to see in the SAP Continuous Influence session

See you again soon for the 2412 release update!

entry.title: Unlocking Value with New Tiers and Pricing for Document Information Extraction Premium Edition
entry.content:
We hope this message finds you well. We have some exciting updates to share regarding our service that are designed to enhance your experience and provide even greater value.

Price Adjustment and New Tiering Concept

As part of our ongoing commitment to delivering cost-effective solutions, we have introduced a new pricing structure along with a tiering concept for our premium edition of the service. This change is particularly beneficial for high-volume use cases, offering more attractive pricing options for our valued customers.

The new pricing structure applies to the service plan: premium_edition. The metric is blocks of 100 documents per month:

- Up to 5 blocks (500 documents)

- Up to 500 blocks (50,000 documents)

- Up to 1,000 blocks (100,000 documents)

- From 1,000 blocks onwards (100,000+ documents)

These new tiers make it easier for you to leverage the cutting-edge large language model technology in a secure and reliable manner, adhering to SAP product, security, and DPP standards. Please check the price updates in SAP Discovery Center.

Key Values of Our Service

We'd also like to remind you of the key benefits our Document Information Extraction service offers:

- Auto Extraction without Model Training: Automatically extract business-relevant entities from documents without the need for extensive model training.

- Instant Learning Based on User Corrections: Our platform improves in real-time based on your inputs and corrections, ensuring that the service evolves to meet your specific needs.

- Embedded Document Classification: Experience seamless document classification with our embedded capabilities, making your workflow more efficient.

- Expanded Availability in Data Centers: our service is now available in additional center regions to enhance accessibility and performance: Azure Netherlands (Amsterdam), Azure U.S. East (Virginia), Azure Australia (Sydney), AWS Southeast Asia (Singapore).

Note: further deployments will follow until the end of 2024: GCP Germany (Frankfurt), GCP U.S. Iowa (Central), GCP Japan East (Tokyo).

We are continuously working to improve our service offerings, and we appreciate your support and the continuous feedback that we are receiving from you.

Thank you for being a valued member of our community. Should you have any questions or require further information, please do not hesitate to reach out.

entry.title: Re: Error with context.html file
entry.content: I'm glad you figured it out! I always just use a folder, and Insert File to put the context.html into the folder. The link would be https://[ManagerURL]/pub/[workareaID]/GR_[folderUID]/context.html . I just replace the context.html every time I generate it. The folder never changes. If you load the context.html as a Media Object with its own M_ UID, then you have to be careful to just Replace Media on the existing Media Object, which replaces the guts of the Media Object without changing its UID and breaking the link from the SM30.

entry.title: SAC Excel Add-In BEx Query as Data Source Not Found Error
entry.content:
Hi All, I am doing a PoC to convert BEx Analyser workbooks to SAC Excel Add-in workbooks. As part of that PoC, I am using tunnel live SAC connections in SAC excel add-in to directly connect to BEx queries. I managed to add a table successfully based on the BEx queries in my excel however after sometime, when I tried changing the variables, I started to get this error below.  BEx query name hidden for security purposes

I can still add a new table in a new tab based on the queries for which I get the error above. However as soon as I log out or close the excel, on reopening I get above errors. The Prompts field is also greyed out.

refresh only allows me to clear data

I have been told that nothing has been changed with my access and hence this issue is quite bizarre. Please let me know if anyone has faced this and how you resolved it.

Thanks

Regards

Tanisha

entry.title: SAP GUI MFA with Microsoft Entra (Part II): Integration with Microsoft Entra Private Access
entry.content:
Part I of this two-part blog post series explains how to setup Multi-Factor-Authentication (MFA) for SAP GUI with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly known as Microsoft Azure Active Directory, AAD) and SAP Secure Login Service (SLS) for SAP GUI. In this scenario, SLS issues a short-lived X.509 client certificate after successful authentication of the user at the SAP Cloud Services Identity Authentication (IAS) tenant. The IAS tenant acts as a SAML 2.0 proxy and forwards the authentication request to the user's Microsoft Entra ID tenant where MFA is enforced using Microsoft Entra Conditional Access (CA). With the X.509 client certificate, SAP GUI establishes a Secure Network Communications (SNC) connection to the SAP Application Server (AS) ABAP to single sign-on (SSO) the user.

This blog post describes an alternative MFA-solution for SAP GUI based on the Kerberos protocol. It uses Microsoft Entra Private Access (PA) instead of SLS and IAS to integrate with Entra ID and Entra CA. Entra PA, and its accomanying service Entra Internet Access, are unified under Global Secure Access (GSA) in the Microsoft Entra admin center. Both enforce an identity-centric Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) strategy. With ZTNA, access is granted per user to specific services or applications, whereas traditional technologies for secure network access, such as Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), grant access to an entire network. VPNs operate at the OSI network layer with protocols such as IPSec. A ZTNA solution like Entra PA runs on the application layer and requires a software application, the Microsoft Entra Global Secure Access (GSA) client, to be installed on the user's devices. The GSA client implements an NDIS 6.0 lightweight filter (LWF) network driver to route any traffic to internal and external applications based on centrally defined access rules at the company's Entra ID tenant level. GSA deeply integrates with Entra CA to secure the access with modern authentication methods to those apps, and can add further controls as needed, such as requiring MFA as presented in this tutorial, allowing access only from a device that fulfills corporate compliance requirements, or detecting sign-in risks like access from atypical locations. 

Figure 1 shows the end-to-end authentication and data flow with PA. Although this blog post follows the same scenario as part I for a user accessing the on-premise SAP system with SAP GUI from a workstation connected to the same (corporate) network, this setup can also be applied to a remote access scenario where the workstation is only connected to the Internet and access to the internal SAP system must be secured.

Figure 1: SAP GUI MFA with Global Secure Access

 

  1. The user Jane Smith logs on to her workstation with her local domain credentials. The workstation is joined to the local AD and to the Entra ID tenant (aka hybrid-joined). By logging in to the local domain, Jane's workstation obtains a Kerberos ticket-granting ticket (TGT) from the AD Domain Controller (DC). The TGT is a special type of Kerberos ticket that can only be used to obtain other tickets from the DC's Ticket Granting Service (TGS) for other kerberized resources and services such as the SAP system.
  2. Jane launches the SAP Logon pad and double-clicks on the system entry for the SAP system with SID A4H. SNC is enabled for this system with a service principal registered in AD using the recommended User Principal Name (UPN) KerberosA4H and Service Principal Name (SPN) SAP/A4H which uniquely identifies the SAP system in Kerberos service tickets for single sign-on (SSO) requested by users. The SAP system has SNC configured with the service principal's credentials as described in steps 1 to 27 of this related blog post.
  3. SAP GUI processes the SNC-protected logon to the SAP system with SAP Secure Login Client (SLC). This requires a new Kerberos service ticket (ST) from the DC for Jane to single sign-on to the SAP system. Without PA, SLC would bypass Entra ID and request the ST for the SAP system from the DC with a Kerberos ticket-granting service request (TGS_REQ) message.
  4. With PA, the locally installed GSA client intercepts the SNC connection on port 3200 based on filter rules, called application segments, defined by GSA applications registered in the Entra ID tenant. These GSA apps represent the resources on the corporate network and control access to those internal apps by specifying the IP address or full-qualified domain name (FQDN), port number and transport layer protocol (TCP or UDP) of the network traffic that you want to intercept with PA. To secure SNC traffic to the SAP system A4H in figure 1, the GSA administrator registers the corresponding enterprise application SAP A4H with an application segment for its IP address 192.168.99.1, SNC port number 3200, and TCP as the transport protocol. The SAP A4H enterprise application is also configured as the target resource in the CA policy that enforces MFA for Jane Smith when she accesses this system. All Kerberos-related traffic (ports 88, 389, 464 and 123) to the DC (IP address 192.168.99.10) using transport protocols TCP and/or UDP gets intercepted based on the application segments defined by the second enterprise application in this scenario, "Domain Conroller". All segments from "SAP A4H" and "Domain Controller" are published to the GSA client(s) on the corporate workstation(s) as the GSA forwarding profile that gets synchronized regularly by a local policy retrieval service.
  5. The GSA client forwards the SNC connection request via Entra PA service to the Entra ID tenant for authentication. With Entra Cloud Sync, Jane's corporate user account and password hash in AD are securely synchronized to the Cloud, and she can SSO with her hybrid identity to her tenant from her hybrid-joined device.
  6. Upon successful authenication, Entra enforces the second authentication factor as defined by a CA policy that has the Global Secure Access application for the SAP A4H system defined as its target resource, and has a grant control set to Passwordless MFA which trigger MFA for every new login request to the SAP system.
  7. GSA client opens a new browser window on Jane's workstation to start the MFA process using passwordless sign-in from Jane's Authenticator app on her mobile device. 
  8. Entra shows a message in the browser window that asks Jane to tap a number in her Authenticator app and complete the sign-in process by providing her PIN or biometric. No username or password is asked for at any time in the authentication flow. 
    Note: Jane authenticated at her hybrid-joined workstation in step 1 with her corporate username and password. If she would have used a stronger authentication method such as Windows Hello for Business that uses biometric data, Entra CA would consider this as strong enough and would not re-enforce authentication with Jane's Authenticator app.
  9. Entra reports the successful completion of the MFA sign-in process back to the GSA client.
  10. SLC requests the ST for the SAP system with an TGS_REQ Kerberos message to the DC's Ticket Granting Service (TGS) on port 88, which gets also intercepted by the GSA Client based on application segments defined by the "Domain Controller" enterprise application. 
  11. GSA Client forwards the Kerberos TGS request to Entra PA.
  12. Since MFA has already been enforced and successfully completed by the previous SNC request, no further intervention from Entra CA is needed. Instead, Entra PA forwards the TGS request to the DC on the corporate network via the Entra private network connector. This agent establishes a secured tunnel from the corporate network over ports 80 and 443 to the Entra PA service and routes the in- and outbound network traffic to all private resources defined by the Global Secure Access enterprise applications. Similar to the SAP Cloud Connector, there is no need to open inbound ports through the corporate firewall because traffic flows both ways once the connection is established. For a test setup, one connector instance on the DC is sufficient. However, in a complex production landscape with more on-premise apps, multiple connector instances can be organized into connector groups for high availability and load balancing, with each group handling traffic to specific applications.
  13. Entra private network connector forwards the TGS request for Jane's SAP Kerberos ticket to the TGS on the DC. It replies with a TGS_REP Kerberos response containing the ticket (for more details on the Kerberos protocol, see also this related blog post)
  14. The TGS_REP message gets forwarded to the SLC via Entra PA and the GSA Client.
  15. With the Kerberos ticket, SAP GUI and SLC can establish the SNC connection and single sign-on user Jane to the SAP system.
  16. Again, all traffic from the GSA client-controlled workstation on port 3200 is routed via Entra PA service and the private network connector on the DC.
  17. Entra private network connector routes SNC communication to the SAP system where Jane can be single signed-on. This requires that Jane's user name in the Kerberos ticket (jsmith@corp.bestrun.com) is correctly mapped to her SAP user account (JSMITH).

Prerequisites

Similar to part I of this blog series, the following prerequisites should be met before starting to implement this scenario:

  • A Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 or Entra Suite tenant with an administrative user assigned to the Entra built-in roles Conditional Access Administrator, Security Administrator, or Global Administrator. The domain name of the Entra tenant used in this tutorial is bestruncorp.onmicrosoft.com. If you can get a free P2 trial tenant with a Microsoft 365 Developer license.
  • A valid license for Microsoft Entra Private Access. You can request a 30-day trial license through the Microsoft 365 admin center.
  • A valid SAP license for enabling SNC-based SSO on the backend system and using SLC on the user's workstation
  • A non-administrator account used for testing the scenario. In this tutorial, Jane Smith has the account with user name jsmith@corp.bestrun.com in AD, jsmith@bestruncorp.onmicrosoft.com in Entra ID, and JSMITH in the SAP system. 
  • Administrative access to an SAP Application Server ABAP for testing the scenario. The system should be configured for SNC following steps 1 to 27 as described in this related tutorial. If you don't have a development system available you can easily setup one with the ABAP Cloud Developer Trial on Docker.
  • Administrative access to an AD DC and a domain-joined workstation for simulating the corporate network. In my lab environment used for this tutorial, both systems run as Hyper-V VMs and are configure according to the table below:

System

Configuration

Domain Controller (DC)

  • Windows Server 2019 or later
  • Active Directory Domain Services (AD DS role). Installing the AD DS role and promoting a Windows Server to a domain controller is documented here. The domain name used in this tutorial is corp.bestrun.com (NetBIOS: CORP), but you can also choose any other name.
  • Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync: Follow these steps to download and install the on-premises agent on the DC.

Workstation

  • Windows 10 Pro or later, hybrid-joined
  • SAP GUI 7.60 or later
  • SAP Secure Login Client 3.0

The GSA on-prem software components, the Microsoft Entra Private Network Connector and the Global Secure Access client, will be installed on the DC and workstation in the following steps.

Ready? Then let's get started with activating PA for the Entra tenant 🚀

Activate Entra Private Access

StepDescriptionScreenshot
1.1

Login as the administrator to the Domain Controller

1.2

 

Open to the Entra Admin Center at https://entra.microsoft.com and login as the tenant administrator. Select Global Secure Access from the navigation menu.

Click the Activate button.

Wait for the message that your tenant onboarding has completed successfully.

1.3

Select Connect -> Traffic Forwarding from the navigation menu.

Activate the switch for the Private access profile. 

1.4Confirm the dialog box with OK.

Install the Microsoft Entra Private Network Connector

Upon successful activation of the PA profile in the tenant, download and install the Microsoft Entra Private Network Connector on the DC. The DC acts as the connector server for the PA service in this scenario. It runs the private network connector which you will download and install in the following steps for tunneling Kerberos and SNC network traffic to the SAP system and the DC.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
2.1

Select Connect -> Connectors from the navigation menu.

Click the Download connector service button.

2.2Accept the terms to start the download.
2.3

Double-click the download file (MicrosoftEntraPrivateNetworkConnectorInstaller.exe) to start the installation of the Private network connector.

Activate the checkbox to accept the license terms and click Install.

2.4

During the installation process you will be asked to login to your Entra ID tenant.

Login as the tenant administrator.

2.5After completion of the connector installation click Restart.
2.6

After the DC has restarted, login to the Entra Admin Center as the tenant administrator.

Select Global Secure Access -> Connect -> Connectors to verify the status of the newly installed connector. It should be assigned to the Default connector group and in state Active.

Install the Global Secure Access client

Continue the GSA setup by installing the Global Secure Access client on the user's workstation. For an automated deployment of the client in a larger production landscape, see the instructions here. 

StepDescriptionScreenshot
3.1Login as user Jane Smith (JSMITH) to the corp.bestun.com (CORP) domain from the workstation.
3.2

Open to the Entra Admin Center and login as the tenant administrator.

Select Global Secure Access -> Connect -> Client download from the navigation menu.

Click Download Client for Windows 10/11.

3.3

Run the GlobalSecureAccessClient.exe installer.

Activate the license terms and conditions checkbox and click Install. Installing the GSA client requires local administrator rights.

 

3.4Upon successful installation, click Sign in.
3.5Login to GSA with your test user's Entra ID account (e.g. jsmith@bestruncorp.onmicrosoft.com)

Configure SAP for Private Access

Upon successful installation of the required software components in the corporate network, it is now time to configure the first GSA application representing the SAP system on-premise, and add the required application segment to it for SNC. You also need to grant access to the test user by assigning Jane Smith to the app.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
4.1

As the tenant admin in the Entra Admin Center (https://entra.microsoft.com), go to Global Secure Access -> Applications -> Enterprise applications in the navigation menu.

Click New application.

4.2

Enter a name for the new Global Secure Access application, for example "SAP A4H".

Click Add application segment.

4.3

Enter the following settings for the new application segment:

  • Destination type: IP address
  • IP address: The IP address of the SAP system in the corporate network, for example 192.168.99.1
  • Ports: 32<NN>. Replace <NN> with the instance number of your SAP system, for example 3200
  • Protocol: TCP

Click Apply.

4.4Click Save.
4.5

Click on Global Secure Access -> Applications -> Enterprise applications in the navigation menu.

Click on the name to select the newly created Global Secure Access application "SAP A4H" from the list.

4.6

Select Users and groups from the navigation menu.

Click Add user/group.

4.7

Click None Selected.

4.8

In the Search field, enter the name of your test user, e.g. Jane Smith, and select the user from the results by activating the checkbox.

Click Select.

4.9

Click Assign.

Configure Kerberos for Private Access

Continue with the application setup for Entra PA by adding the "Domain Controller" GSA enterprise app to intercept Kerberos traffic to the DC.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
5.1

Click on Global Secure Access -> Applications -> Enterprise applications in the navigation menu.

Click New application.

 

5.2

Enter a name, for example "Domain Controller".

Click Add application segment.

 

5.3

Enter the following settings for the new application segment:

  • Destination type: IP address
  • IP address: The IP address of the DC in the corporate network, for example 192.168.99.10
  • Ports: 88
  • Protocol: Select TCP and UDP

Click Apply.

5.4

Repeat the previous step for the following three application segments, which have all the destination type "IP address" and same IP address (your DC's address, e.g. 192.168.99.10):

  • Ports: 389, Protocol: UDP
  • Ports: 464, Protocol: TCP and UDP
  • Ports: 123, Protocol: UDP

Click Save.

5.5

Click on Global Secure Access -> Applications -> Enterprise applications in the navigation menu.

Click on the name to select the newly created Global Secure Access application "Domain Controller" from the list.

5.6

Select Users and groups from the navigation menu.

Click Add user/group.

5.7

Click None Selected.

5.8

In the Search field, enter the name of your test user, e.g. Jane Smith, and select the user from the results by activating the checkbox.

Click Select.

5.9

Click Assign.

Configure MFA for SAP GUI with Entra Conditional Access

Setup MFA for the user in Entra by creating a CA policy and assigning it to the "SAP A4H" GSA application for the SAP system.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
6.1

Select Global Secure Access -> Applications -> Enterprise applications from the navigation menu.

Click on the name to select the newly created Global Secure Access application "SAP A4H" from the list.

6.2From the enterprise application navigation menu, select Security -> Conditional Access.
6.3Click New policy.
6.4Enter a name for the new policy, for example "SAP GUI MFA", and click on the link in the Users section.
6.5Activate the checkbox for Users and groups
6.6

In the Search field, enter the name of your test user, e.g. Jane Smith, and select the user from the results by activating the checkbox.

Click Select.

6.7Click on the link in the Access controls -> Grant section.
6.8

Keep the default selection for Grant access, and activate the checkbox for Require authentication strength.

From the drop-down box, select Passwordless MFA.

Click Select.

6.9

Switch to On in the Enable policy options.

Click Create.

Setup private DNS for Kerberos SSO

To support SSO with Kerberos, the GSA client must be able to resolve internal (private) DNS names with the corporate domain suffix (corp.bestrun.com in this setup). 

StepDescriptionScreenshot
7.1

Run the command ipconfig /all on the DC or workstation host to find the DNS suffix for your corporate network.

Copy the value (here corp.bestrun.com) of Primary Dns suffix from the output.

 

7.1

Select Global Secure Access -> Applications -> Quick Access from the navigation menu.

Switch to the Private DNS tab.

Activate the Enable Private DNS checkbox and click Add DNS suffix.

7.2

Paste the DNS suffix from step 7.1 for your corporate network in the DNS suffix field.

Click Add.

7.3Select Users and Groups from the navigation menu.
7.4Click None Selected.


7.5

In the Search field, enter the name of your test user, e.g. Jane Smith, and select the user from the results by activating the checkbox.

Click Select.

7.6Click Assign.

Setting up MFA with Phone Sign-in for the test user

Since you selected the Passwordless MFA option for the Required authentication strength in the previously created Conditional Access policy, you now need to setup the test user's account accordingly. The following steps assume that there is no MFA authentication methods registered yet for the user. If so, make sure that Phone Sign-In is activated in the user's Authenticator app. Otherwise just follow the next steps. 

StepDescriptionScreenshot
8.1

As the test user on the workstation, login to the My account portal at https://myaccount.microsoft.com.

On the Security info tile, click UPDATE INFO.

8.2Click Add sign-in method.
8.3

Select Authenticator app from the drop-down list.

Click Add.

8.4Click Next.
8.5Click Next.
8.6On the user's phone, launch the Authenticator app. Click + to add a new account.
8.7

Select Work or school account.

Click Scan QR code.

8.8Scan the QR code displayed in the My access portal with your device camera.
8.9

Go back to the My access portal.

Click Next.

8.10A random number is shown in the My access portal to approve the new account.
8.11

A notification will pop-up in the Authenticator app. Enter the number shown in the previous step.

Click Yes.

8.12Upon successful approval, click Next.
8.13

Select the newly added account in the Authenticator app.

Select Enable phone sign-in from the menu.

8.14Click Continue.
8.15

Enter your test users domain password to authenticate with the Entra ID tenant.

Click Sign in.

8.16

Entra ID login will show a random number.

A few seconds later, the notification from the Authenticator app will pop-up.

Enter the number and click Yes.

8.17You have successfully enabled password-less MFA with phone sign-in for your test user's account.

Configure SNC mapping for the user in the SAP system

Similar to Certificate-based SSO with SNC in part I of the blog series, Kerberos-based SSO to the SAP system also requires a mapping of the SAP user account to the user's name in the corporate domain.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
9.1

Login to the SAP backend with your SAP administrator user (e.g. DEVELOPER if you are using the Docker image).

Go to User Maintenance (SU01).

Enter your test user's name (e.g. JSMITH).

Click Create User (F8) if the user account doesn't exist. Otherwise, click Change (Shift+F6), and continue with step 9.4.

9.2

Enter the Last Name and First Name on the Address tab.

Switch to the Logon Data tab.

9.3

Enter a Password for the new user.

9.4

Switch to the SNC tab.

In the SNC name field, enter your test user's name in the corporate AD domain following the format "p:CN=<user@domain-dns-suffix>", e.g. "p:CN=JSMITH@CORP.BESTRUN.COM".

Click Save.

Test the scenario

You are now ready to verify the scenario 🚀!

StepDescriptionScreenshot
10.1Login as the test user jsmith to the local domain (CORP) on the workstation.
10.2Hover over the GSA system tray icon to check that the status is "connected".
10.3Right-click on the GSA system tray icon and select Advanced diagnostics from the context menu.
10.4Switch to the Health check tab. Ensure that the status bar shows that All checks are successful.
10.5

Switch to the Forwarding profile tab and expand the Private access rules section.

Check that all rules defined by the application segments of the "SAP A4H" and "Domain Controller" GSA enterprise applications were synchronized by the local GSA policy retrieval service.

You also see additional rules for tunneling DNS on port 53 based on your Private DNS settings. 

10.6

Switch to the Traffic tab.

Click Start collecting to record a trace of the intercepted SNC, Kerberos and DNS network traffic from the local workstation to the Entra PA service.

10.7

Start the SAP Logon Pad. 

Right-click on your connection entry for the SAP system.

Select Properties from the context menu.

10.8

Switch to the Network tab.

Activate the checkbox for Activate Secure Network Communication.

Enter the SNC Name of your SAP backend. The name can be looked up on the backend with transaction RZ10 and the profile parameter snc/identity/as.

Choose Maximum security settings available.

Click OK.

10.9Right-click on the connection entry for the SAP system and select SNC Logon with Single Sign-On.
10.10

SAP GUI now starts to establish the SNC connection, which gets intercepted by the GSA client (step 3 & 4 in figure 1) that requires the user to complete the multi-factor authentication process.

On the Global Secure Access Client window, click Sign in.

10.11

In the Entra ID login page, enter your test user's corporate password.

Click Sign in.

10.12Entra Conditional Access now requires the second factor by prompting the user to enter the number shown on the login page in her Authenticator app on the mobile phone.
10.13

The Authenticator app opens on the mobile phone. Upon successfully unlocking the app (e.g. with Face ID), the user has to enter the number shown in the previous step.

Click Yes.

10.14

The login process completes and the test user is successfully single signed-on to the SAP system.

10.15

To see the network traces, go back to the GSA Advanced diagnostics window. The Traffic tab shows the recorded network traffic over the tunneled connections to the DC (port 88) and the SAP system (port 3200).

With the user logged on in SAP GUI, the Connection status of the the tunneled SNC connection is still Active.

Congratulations on completing the tutorial for SAP GUI MFA with Entra Private Access 🏆🥇!

entry.title: Multi Assignment Details in Variable Pay Statement
entry.content:
Dear Experts, 

Regarding Variable Pay statement, If an employee is having multi assignment within the bonus cycle, Business wanted to display the details from assignment section and business goal section to variable pay statement. Details are, 

Assignment1

Assignment Basis   (assignmentBasis)
Proration   (assignmentProration)
Salary   
Performance Cluster  
Differentiation Factor   (goalResultsPayoutPercent)
TargetBonus   (Ctarget)

Assignment2

Assignment Basis   (assignmentBasis)
Proration   (assignmentProration)
Salary   
Performance Cluster  
Differentiation Factor   (goalResultsPayoutPercent)
TargetBonus   (Ctarget)

 

Best Regards, Mathi S

entry.title: Re: Multi Assignment Details in Variable Pay Statement
entry.content:
Hi @mathivanans 

Thanks for posting here. 

Unfortunately it is not possible to build this in standard, assignment level information can only be pulled using a custom statement (xsl development).

If you have a reporting expert or anybody with software development skills (xsl isn't very complicated for them - I saw a customer IT team build a multi pagecomplex custom statement in 24h) there are some useful blog on the community that can help them get started quickly (search for "custom compensation statement") and if you get stuck then you can ping us here with your questions (please search in community first) and we can reach out to SAP reporting experts that build them when we are engaged on customer implementations.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Proration and Number Formats in Variable Pay
entry.content:
Hi @dorota_g 

I won't have time to test on this any time soon due to other commitments. 

Please try #,##0.000000 then run bonus calculation again and relaunch the worksheet (mandatory steps after you change number formats to retest).

If it works as I would expect and you can see a lot of decimals, then start reverting back until you have the number of decimals you want.

I wish Variable Pay was more of a predictable module with clear answers to all questions but even when I am confronted with these issues I apply the steps above.

Good luck and please let me know.

Xavier

entry.title: How to get the API Key for S4 HANA Cloud trial (Not for the public sandbox).
entry.content:
Hey all, I've been struggling to find how to get the API key for my trial of S4 HANA Cloud.

I've only found how to retrieve the API Key that let's you hit this url: https://sandbox.api.sap.com
But where do I get the key for my S4 HANA Cloud trial instance that has a url of this style: https://myXXXXX.s4hana.ondemand.com/ ?

I'd appreciate a lot some help, also if you can mention how I'd get the API Key for a production environment as well, would be helpful in advance. Cheers.

entry.title: Re: How to Edit Multiple Components Simultaneously in SAP Build Apps’ Logic Canvas?
entry.content:
Hello @Dan_Wroblewski, thank you so much for the quick response!

1) At first, I controlled the visibility of components through several page variables. However, after an update, whenever a variable starts as “false,” the component becomes invisible in the user interface, which greatly complicates the development process. In my case, each page has a variety of components that sometimes need to be displayed and sometimes hidden. When they start off invisible, not being able to see them directly in the UI slows down the work. That’s why I chose to use the “Hide component” and “Show component” features, which don’t have this issue. The new challenge is that I now use dozens of them per page, and if it were possible to edit their properties to control multiple components at once, the logical flow would be much simpler and more organized.

2) Exactly, I’m referring to the “Hide component” and “Show component” features. It would be extremely useful to add more than one input, allowing the visibility of multiple components to be managed simultaneously, making the process more efficient.

entry.title: Introducing the SAP Cloud SDK for AI (Java) 🎉
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce the initial release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI for Java! Only a few weeks ago we already announced the release of the JavaScript/TypeScript variant. Similarly, this SDK for Java enables convenient integration of generative AI capabilities within your SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) applications and allows you to utilize the Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core.

For SAP BTP developers, the AI SDK is crafted to simplify AI integration and enhance application impact. With features that optimize deployment, improve content safety, and facilitate model orchestration, the SDK lets you bring advanced AI functionality to your applications swiftly and with minimal setup. Whether you need adaptable workflows, secure data handling, or smooth generative model integration, the SDK equips you with robust tools to embed AI-powered features in your SAP BTP solutions.

This post introduces the main modules and their features.

 

AI Core - Setup and Usage

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.sap.ai.sdk</groupId>
  <artifactId>core</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>

Automate tasks such as creating AI Core artifacts, configurations, and deployments, executing batch inference jobs, as well as managing Docker registries and object storage for training data. The core module provides tools for workflow and scenario management within SAP AI Core.

  • Artifact management: register and organize datasets and model artifacts.
  • Configuration management: set up configurations for various models and use cases.
  • Deployment management: deploy AI models and manage their lifecycle within SAP AI Core.

Example SDK code: Create a deployment in SAP AI Core.

var api = new DeploymentApi();
var resourceGroupId = "default";
var request = AiDeploymentCreationRequest.create().configurationId("12345-123-123-123-123456abcdefg");

AiDeploymentCreationResponse deployment = api.create(resourceGroupId, request);
String id = deployment.getId();
AiExecutionStatus status = deployment.getStatus();
You can learn more about the SDK's capabilities for SAP AI Core in the public repository guide.
 

AI Core - Orchestration

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.sap.ai.sdk</groupId>
  <artifactId>orchestration</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>
This orchestration module lets you use the Generative AI Hub Orchestration Service with templating, content filtering, and data masking from within your applications. The Orchestration Service streamlines AI interactions while ensuring adherence to content safety guidelines.
  • Templating: Build dynamic prompts with placeholders to tailor AI interactions to user inputs.
  • Content Filtering: Apply filters to maintain compliance with content safety guidelines.
  • Data Masking: Anonymize and pseudonymize sensitive data.
  • Grounding: Add external data sources for contextually relevant information (planned for Q1 2025).

Example SDK code: Write a simple chat completion.

var client = new OrchestrationClient();
var config = new OrchestrationModuleConfig().withLlmConfig(OrchestrationAiModel.GPT_4O);
var prompt = new OrchestrationPrompt("Hello world! Why is this phrase so famous?");
var result = client.chatCompletion(prompt, config);

String messageResult = result.getContent();
You can learn more about the SDK's capabilities for Orchestration Service in the public repository guide.
 

AI Core - Foundation Models

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.sap.ai.sdk.foundationmodels</groupId>
  <artifactId>openai</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>
The openai module, along with other modules in the com.sap.ai.sdk.foundationmodels group, enables streamlined access to specific generative AI models available through the Generative AI Hub. The module provides a simplified interface focused on direct model interactions, ideal for developers who require direct access to foundation models for inference and embedding requests without additional orchestration features.

Currently only openai is supported. Please open a feature request, if you need direct LLM access for other foundation models.

Example SDK code: Write a simple chat completion.

var result = 
  OpenAiClient.forModel(GPT_35_TURBO)
    .withSystemPrompt("You are a helpful AI")
    .chatCompletion("Hello World! Why is this phrase so famous?");

String resultMessage = result.getContent();
You can learn more about the SDK's capabilities for foundation models and OpenAI specific features in the public repository guide.
 

Getting Started

You will need Java 17 or higher. Spring Boot or SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) as a framework is recommended, but not required. To explore these packages further, check out our sample project, which shows the usage of the various SDK packages.

 

Support and Feedback

We’d love your feedback on this first release! For support or to share your ideas, feel free to open an issue on GitHub.

 

Latest News

  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI for JavaScript released in October 2024.
  • Visit sap.com/ai and explore our portfolio
  • Explore the available AI capabilities on SAP Discovery Center.
  • Discover the latest announcements in the SAP TechEd Press Release and the SAP TechEd News Guide.
  • Review the SAP Road Map Explorer for a detailed view of upcoming product innovations.
  • Join the SAP Community page to connect with experts and share knowledge.

entry.title: NWBC for HTML Will Be Discontinued
entry.content:
The NWBC for HTML (NetWeaver Business Client for HTML) has been on a long journey and it is time to bring this to an end. Starting with S/4HANA 2025 it will be discontinued. But you will not be kept alone—there are two alternatives you can choose from:

  • Fiori Launchpad (FLP): Now this is the right moment to switch to FLP.
  • SAP Business Client (SAP BC) aka NWBC for Desktop: This is a desktop application for Microsoft Windows in which you can use the old roles and where you have some advantages over the NWBC for HTML: 
    • Multiple tabs to work with various web-based applications and Dynpro transactions in parallel 
    • Favorites 
    • Context-sensitive sidepanel 
    • Favourites 
    • Overview of most viewed applications and recently used applications 
    • Accessing Dynpro transactions via SAPGUI for Windows 
    • Harmonized UI, including a themed shell 

On this journey the names of the products changed a little bit. Therefore, it is understandable that there might be some confusion. Let's try to clarify this:

  • In the beginning there was the NWBC for HTML and the NWBC for Desktop. Talking about the NWBC was sometimes ambiguous because of the different products with nearly the same name.
  • With version 6.0 the NWBC for Desktop was renamed to SAP Business Client for Desktop (SAP BC) while the NWBC for HTML stayed with its name.
  • So now there is the SAP Business Client for Desktop (in the meantime with version 8.00) and the NWBC for HTML.
  • If you hear about the discontinuation of the NWBC this is only about the NWBC for HTML, not about the SAP BC! The SAP BC will continue.

If you have many custom roles defined for your NWBC you can think about switching to the SAP BC because all the old roles can be used there. You don't need to change them—just check the additional features which are perhaps helpful for you and your users, e.g. own Menu entries, Search Providers or Side Panels which can provide additional information to existing applications.

A disadvantage could be that the SAP BC is a Windows application which has to be installed on every relevant machine. Find information about the SAP BC in the SAP Help Portal.

The other option is switching to the SAP Fiori Launchpad. This is the most current shell of SAP. It runs in the browser, so you don't have to install something on every computer in your environment. If you use custom roles, you will probably need to make yourself familiar with the new role concept and create new roles for your environment. In the SAP Help Portal you will find more information about this.

Be aware that you also can run the FLP within the SAP BC. More about this you can also find in the SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: Success factors for custom AI use cases: from the idea to the productive phase
entry.content:
The question of whether or not the use of AI in a company can be a value-added booster has long been answered - in fact, it is almost negligent not to consider the use of AI in one's company. Nevertheless, our experience shows that only a portion of the AI use case ideas considered for implementation ultimately makes it to the productive phase. At SAP, we understand that the introduction of AI use cases is a challenging journey into often unfamiliar territory. This makes it even more important to have an approach that provides clear orientation.

A lifecycle stage model for categorizing AI use cases is a critical tool for navigating the challenges of AI adoption and ensuring successful implementation. It offers several advantages: Many initiatives fail due to technical difficulties, unclear goals, or a lack of user acceptance. By applying a staged approach, resources can be allocated more effectively, risks identified early, and focus directed toward promising projects. This ensures that AI use cases are efficiently developed, from the initial idea to impactful deployment. Additionally, it serves as an excellent methodology for monitoring whether there are sufficient ideas and use cases in the early stages to meet company KPIs, such as achieving a specific number of go-live use cases each year.

These insights are based on experience within a successful AI program at a client and are specifically true for custom AI use cases. It’s important to note that “Embedded” or “Joule AI” use cases can also be classified within the same stage structure, but it might be possible to start with them directly at a later stage or skip certain stages in between.

Introducing the five stages lifecycle model - from exploration to roll-out
As illustrated in Fig. 1, the lifecycle stage model, consisting of five stages, can be divided into two phases. The early stages require relatively fewer resources and less technological investment and focus on evaluating whether a use case is viable for productive deployment. If these early stages are successful and a use case is considered beneficial, the mature stages focused on productive use can follow.

Figure 1: Five stages lifecycle model

Early stages: From the idea to the prototype
In the early stages of an AI use case, the focus is on defining the user group, clarifying the intended benefits, and identifying potential risks. In addition, consideration is given to its integration into end-to-end processes. This structured approach ensures a lean start with minimal use of resources and reduces the risk of unnecessary expenditure. The specific characteristics of each stage are described below:

1. Exploration
In the exploration phase, use cases are evaluated based on their potential benefits, effort, and risk. The primary task in this phase is to identify relevant and feasible AI use cases. However, this assessment is initially only a rough estimate, without a clear concept, an examination of the underlying data quality, and an analysis of the end-to-end process.

2. Low Fidelity Prototype
The focus here is on the concept. Workshops, such as design thinking, are used to define the target group, benefits and process integration. The aim is to assess the fundamental feasibility, viability and desirability. An essential component is to obtain a clear picture of the underlying data quality in order to identify potential obstacles at an early stage. Additionally, it provides an excellent opportunity to rethink and transform business processes through the use of AI.

3. Functional Prototype
Based on the findings, a functional prototype has been developed to evaluate its technical feasibility. Furthermore, the AI model must be trained and improved, which is usually a key challenge. During the technical implementation, however, the focus is less on perfecting of the prototype but more on validating the concept of the LoFi prototype. This step ensures efficient resource management and prepares the transition to a possible, more resource-intensive productive use.

From validation to scaling: the path to productive use
If the early stages lead to the conclusion of a positive use case, the mature stages follow, which are characterized by productive use. This refers to use in day-to-day business. How these two phases of productive use differ is described as follows:  

4. Pilot
The aim here is to achieve productive use, which requires greater effort, perfection and technical robustness. Additionally, process integration and change management represent the key challenges in this phase. However, meaningful further development in these phases only takes place if the benefits of the use case have been sufficiently demonstrated in the previous phases (e.g. through the functional prototype).

5. Roll-out
The roll-out throughout the company takes place as soon as the use case has been successfully tested in the previous phases and confirmed to be useful for productive use, with a continued focus on change management and ensuring a consumer-ready AI. In order to avoid disruptions in day-to-day business, the use case should have been prepared in a stable and reliable manner.

This maturity level structure described above provides many advantages for the stability of your AI use case. You will have identified potential obstacles at an early stage. You will have ensured efficient resource management and you will be prepared to transition to a possible, more resource-intensive productive use. The functional prototype sufficiently demonstrates its use, and when productive, you can be confident that disruptions will be avoided in day-to-day business.

Shaping Your entire AI Program with the Lifecycle Stage Structure
In addition to the already mentioned advantages of a maturity level structure, this structure can provide another important strategic benefit for companies with ambitious AI goals that are developing several AI use cases in parallel.

Being able to observe the lifecycle during the stages enables systematic and successful further development of the entire portfolio.[1] For example, with an overview of the number of use cases in each phase, important strategic AI KPIs - such as the introduction of a certain number of productive use cases each year - can be realistically evaluated. This provides a fantastic overview of whether there are enough use cases in the early-stage pipeline to achieve the company's goal of bringing a certain number of use cases into productive use, considering that only a portion of use cases progress from idea to productive use. If necessary, targeted actions such as exploration workshops to come up with new use cases can be initiated to unlock new potential.

To significantly increase the number of AI use cases that go live, organizations must also prioritize certain critical factors. Success at this level requires more than just structure and ambition - it requires a focus on the fundamental phases of the AI use case lifecycle. In particular, the first three phases - exploration, low-fidelity prototype (LoFi) and functional prototype - are critical to future success. These early phases lay the foundation for overcoming challenges and ensure that the use cases can be deployed smoothly.

The following key success factors, derived from practical examples of custom AI use cases, highlight what organizations must focus on to turn their ambitions into reality:

  • Data understanding and data quality: A solid database and data knowledge are essential for predicting potential difficulties when designing implementation options.
  • Early error culture and agile project management: Trial-and-error approaches help to identify weaknesses at an early stage. Weekly planning and interim goals create flexibility and a clear overview. Agile development should be used especially from the creation of a functional prototype onward in order to enable early testing and react more quickly to new or changing findings.
  • Design thinking: The use of the design thinking methodology when creating a low-fidelity prototype enables user-centered development that identifies weaknesses at an early stage. It also promotes interdisciplinary collaboration and thus ensures creative solutions that simultaneously question the current process and streamline it where necessary.
  • Business involvement: Involving the business at an early stage provides realistic insights and improves subsequent acceptance. Especially when using design thinking, it helps enormously in the creation of personas or the as-is analysis to carry out a well-founded assessment.
  • Standardized assets and test data: Templates for carrying out the methodology and early prepared test data are real accelerators, as they enable the prototype phase to be completed more quickly through smart preparation and follow-up work from the workshops and prevent delays during testing
  • Clear leadership and stakeholder communication: Consistent communication and coordinated project management promote transparency and acceptance, especially in cooperation projects with partners who provide for instance, AI solutions for the respective use case.

Your journey to success
A lifecycle stage structure for AI use cases ensures transparency, efficiency and optimized resource management. It not only helps to bring projects successfully to the productive phase, but also to identify risks and challenges at portfolio level at an early stage. This paves the way for the sustainable and scalable use of AI.

The AI race is already well underway. Don’t miss the chance to approach this transformative topic in a structured manner and unlock the opportunities AI has to offer. Leverage the framework and recommendations outlined in this article to enhance your current or future AI use cases. If you’re finding it challenging to get started or feel stuck along the way, SAP Advisory Services is here to support you. Whether it’s through our use case exploration workshops or our service to develop a tailored AI strategy, we’ll gladly guide you on this exciting journey.

 

[1] AI Strategy Approach | Dr. Norbert Enge | SAP Business Transformation & Architecture

entry.title: OData API Versions: A Comparative Analysis of v2 and v4
entry.content:
1. Data Types and Modeling:

  • OData v2: Supports data types such as DateTime, Time, and Float.

OData v4: Introduces additional data types like Date, TimeOfDay, Single, and Enum. It also enhances the entity model with features like inheritance and containment, offering a more robust data modeling framework.

2. Query Capabilities:

  • OData v2: Allows filtering and selection operations on root entities.

OData v4: Expands these capabilities to include filtering, sorting, and selection on expanded entities. It also supports full-text search and user-defined functions, providing more flexible and powerful querying options.

3. Operation Methods:

  • OData v2: Utilizes the HTTP POST method with the X-HTTP-METHOD: MERGE header for data updates.

OData v4: Employs the HTTP PATCH method for updates, aligning with standard HTTP semantics and simplifying partial updates.

4. Performance and Scalability:

OData v4: Offers delta query support, enabling clients to retrieve only changes since the last query. This reduces data transfer and enhances performance, especially in scenarios with large datasets.

5. Support and Future Enhancements:

SAP is progressively focusing on OData v4 as the preferred standard. Future developments and support are expected to concentrate on v4, making it a more future-proof choice.

6. Advantages of the $expand Query Option in OData v4: The $expand system query option in OData v4 allows clients to include related entities inline within the response, facilitating the retrieval of related data in a single request. This reduces the need for multiple round-trips to the server, thereby improving efficiency.

Example: To retrieve a list of categories along with their associated products:

 

 

 

 

GET /odata/Categories?$expand=Products

 

 

This query fetches all categories and their related products in one response.

 

OData v4 enhances the $expand functionality by allowing nested expansions and the application of query options within the expand clause. For instance, to retrieve categories with only the top 5 most expensive products:

 
GET /odata/Categories?$expand=Products($orderby=Price desc;$top=5)

This query returns each category along with its top 5 most expensive products, sorted in descending order by price.

7. Error Responses: Error responses in OData v2 and v4 are formatted in JSON, but v4 provides a more structured and detailed format, aiding in precise error handling and debugging.

OData v2 Error Response Example:{ "error": { "code": "BadRequest", "message": { "lang": "en-US", "value": "Invalid request." } } }OData v4 Error Response Example:

{
  "error": {
    "code": "BadRequest",
    "message": "Invalid request.",
    "target": "query",
    "details": [
      {
        "code": "InvalidFilter",
        "target": "$filter",
        "message": "The filter expression is invalid."
      }
    ],
    "innererror": {
      "trace": [],
      "context": {}
    }
  }
}

The OData v4 error response includes additional fields such as target, details, and innererror, providing more context and facilitating easier troubleshooting.

Conclusion and Recommendation: If your project requires advanced data types, flexible query capabilities, and improved performance, adopting OData v4 APIs is advisable. However, if your existing systems are compatible with OData v2 and meet your current needs, continuing with v2 is acceptable. Considering future support and enhancements, opting for OData v4 for new projects and developments is a more sustainable approach.

For more detailed information, refer to SAP's OData v4 API Reference Guide and OData v2 API Reference Guide.
